name: Senso-ji Temple
description: Tokyo's oldest temple, founded in 645 AD. Famous for its iconic Kaminarimon (Thunder Gate) with a giant red lantern. The Nakamise shopping street leading to the main hall offers traditional snacks and souvenirs. The five-story pagoda and main hall are beautifully illuminated at night.
access: Asakusa Station (Ginza Line, Tobu Skytree Line) - 5 min walk

name: Tokyo Skytree
description: The tallest tower in Japan at 634 meters, offering panoramic views of Tokyo from two observation decks. The Tembo Deck at 350m and the Tembo Galleria at 450m provide stunning city views. On clear days, you can see Mount Fuji. The base houses Tokyo Solamachi, a large shopping and entertainment complex.
access: Tokyo Skytree Station (Tobu Skytree Line) - direct connection. Oshiage Station (Hanzomon Line) - 1 min walk.
admission: Tembo Deck: 2,100 yen (adult). Tembo Deck + Galleria: 3,100 yen (adult).

name: Meiji Shrine
description: A serene Shinto shrine surrounded by a lush forest of 170,000 trees, dedicated to Emperor Meiji and Empress Shoken. The large torii gates and peaceful walking paths offer a tranquil escape from the bustling city. Popular for traditional Shinto weddings and New Year visits (hatsumode).
access: Harajuku Station (JR Yamanote Line) or Meiji-jingumae Station (Chiyoda/Fukutoshin Line) - 1 min walk
hours: Sunrise to sunset (varies by season, roughly 5:00-18:00)

name: Shibuya Crossing
name_ja: 渋谷スクランブル交差点
description: The world's busiest pedestrian crossing, where up to 3,000 people cross at once during peak times. An iconic symbol of Tokyo's energy. Best viewed from the Shibuya Sky observation deck on the rooftop of Scramble Square, or from the Starbucks on the 2nd floor of the TSUTAYA building overlooking the crossing.
access: Shibuya Station (JR, Ginza/Hanzomon/Fukutoshin Lines, Tokyu, Keio Inokashira) - direct exit

name: Ueno Park & Museums
description: A spacious public park home to several world-class museums, a zoo, and beautiful cherry blossom trees. Major museums include the Tokyo National Museum (Japan's largest), the National Museum of Western Art (UNESCO World Heritage), and the National Museum of Nature and Science. Ueno Zoo houses giant pandas.
access: Ueno Station (JR, Ginza/Hibiya Lines) - 2 min walk
hours: Park: 5:00-23:00. Museums vary (most close Mon, open 9:30-17:00).
admission: Park: Free. Tokyo National Museum: 1,000 yen. Zoo: 600 yen.

name: Tsukiji Outer Market
description: A lively food market with hundreds of stalls and restaurants offering the freshest seafood, sushi, tamagoyaki (Japanese omelette), and street food. While the inner wholesale market moved to Toyosu in 2018, the outer market remains a top destination for food lovers. Best visited in the morning for the freshest selections.
access: Tsukiji Station (Hibiya Line) - 1 min walk. Tsukijishijo Station (Oedo Line) - 1 min walk.
hours: Most shops: 5:00-14:00 (varies). Many shops closed on Sundays and holidays.

name: Akihabara Electric Town
description: The global center of otaku (anime/manga/gaming) culture and electronics. Filled with multi-story electronics shops, anime merchandise stores, maid cafes, and arcades. From retro games to the latest gadgets, Akihabara offers a unique and immersive experience for tech and pop culture enthusiasts.
access: Akihabara Station (JR Yamanote/Sobu Lines, Hibiya Line, Tsukuba Express) - direct exit
hours: Most shops: 10:00-21:00. Some arcades open later.

name: Imperial Palace East Gardens
description: The beautiful former site of Edo Castle's innermost circles, now open to the public as part of the Imperial Palace grounds. Features traditional Japanese gardens, ruins of the castle keep, moats, and seasonal flowers. A peaceful oasis in the heart of Tokyo's business district with rich historical significance.
access: Otemachi Station (Marunouchi/Tozai/Chiyoda/Hanzomon/Mita Lines) - 5 min walk. Tokyo Station - 10 min walk.
hours: 9:00-16:30 (Mar-Oct varies). Closed Mon, Fri, and some holidays.

name: Odaiba
description: A futuristic entertainment district on a man-made island in Tokyo Bay. Features a replica Statue of Liberty, the teamLab Borderless digital art museum, shopping malls like DiverCity (with a life-size Gundam statue), and beautiful Rainbow Bridge views. The beach area offers a unique waterfront atmosphere.
access: Yurikamome Line from Shimbashi Station. Rinkai Line to Tokyo Teleport Station.

name: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden
description: One of Tokyo's largest and most beautiful parks, blending three distinct garden styles: Japanese traditional, English landscape, and French formal. Spanning 58.3 hectares, it is especially popular during cherry blossom season (spring) and autumn foliage. A tranquil retreat next to the bustling Shinjuku district.
access: Shinjuku-gyoenmae Station (Marunouchi Line) - 5 min walk. Shinjuku Station - 10 min walk.
hours: 9:00-16:30 (last entry 16:00). Closed Mon (or next day if Mon is holiday).
